skills.name: Policy Gate Evaluation
skills.what_it_proves: Public policy rules block critical, admin, and prod-write scope before validation moves.
skills.safety_boundary: read-only; humans approve
skills.layman_summary: Runs policy rules: which scenarios are blocked before any pilot.
skills.example_question: Which agent scenarios pass or fail the policy gate?
skills.prompt: Explain the output of the Policy Gate Evaluation harness skill for a hackathon judge. Context: Public policy rules block critical, admin, and prod-write scope before validation moves. Safety: read-only; humans approve

skills.name: Access Request Normalization
skills.what_it_proves: Messy role-level YAML maps to a structured public access-review input.
skills.safety_boundary: no secrets accepted; role-level only
skills.layman_summary: Turns a messy access request into a clean checklist the system can review.
skills.example_question: What did the trial request ask for (GitHub, Slack, Jira)?
skills.prompt: Explain the output of the Access Request Normalization harness skill for a hackathon judge. Context: Messy role-level YAML maps to a structured public access-review input. Safety: no secrets accepted; role-level only

skills.name: DecisionPacket Generation
skills.what_it_proves: A structured access request becomes a schema-backed packet with scope, proof debt, reviewers, and safety state.
skills.safety_boundary: no production-access claim; never approves
skills.layman_summary: Shows the official “should this agent get access?” packet — blocked vs allowed.
skills.example_question: What blocks production access for the support triage bot?
skills.prompt: Summarize the support_triage_agent DecisionPacket for a human reviewer: what is blocked, what proof is missing, and what scoped validation is allowed. Do not claim production access was granted.

skills.name: Proof Debt Extraction
skills.what_it_proves: Missing evidence and unsupported claims stay visible as reviewer work.
skills.safety_boundary: no complete claim without verified evidence
skills.layman_summary: Lists missing proof (owners and what each item unblocks).
skills.example_question: What proof is still missing and who owns it?
skills.prompt: Explain the output of the Proof Debt Extraction harness skill for a hackathon judge. Context: Missing evidence and unsupported claims stay visible as reviewer work. Safety: no complete claim without verified evidence

skills.name: Reviewer Routing
skills.what_it_proves: Review owners and action items are derived from the packet instead of hidden in prose.
skills.safety_boundary: routing is recommendation; never dispatches
skills.layman_summary: Who must sign off next (security, platform, etc.) and what they need to do.
skills.example_question: Which reviewers must act before validation can start?
skills.prompt: Explain the output of the Reviewer Routing harness skill for a hackathon judge. Context: Review owners and action items are derived from the packet instead of hidden in prose. Safety: routing is recommendation; never dispatches

skills.name: Risk-Aware Scenario Differentiation
skills.what_it_proves: Low, medium/high, and critical requests produce materially different review postures.
skills.safety_boundary: read-only; humans approve
skills.layman_summary: Compares low-, medium-, and high-risk agents side by side.
skills.example_question: How is the admin bot different from read-only analytics?
skills.prompt: Explain the output of the Risk-Aware Scenario Differentiation harness skill for a hackathon judge. Context: Low, medium/high, and critical requests produce materially different review postures. Safety: read-only; humans approve

skills.name: Sponsor Evidence Replay
skills.what_it_proves: Sponsor proof slots and sanitized live-evidence fixtures attach to a trial decision while verdict, approvals, grants, writes, and production mutation stay locked.
skills.safety_boundary: dry-run by default; no live writes; sponsor cannot grant access
skills.layman_summary: Sponsor proof slots and sanitized live-evidence fixtures attach to a trial decision while verdict, approvals, grants, writes, and production mutation stay locked.
skills.example_question: What does Sponsor Evidence Replay show for a hackathon judge?
skills.prompt: Explain Sponsor Evidence Replay: where Tavily, Composio, Nebius, and OpenClaw attach proof, and why the sponsors cannot change the decision or grant access.

skills.name: Design Partner Trial Runner
skills.what_it_proves: A role-level trial request becomes a report, packet, and access brief without live credentials.
skills.safety_boundary: no live integration path; humans review
skills.layman_summary: Walks through a design-partner trial request end to end.
skills.example_question: Summarize the support triage trial outcome for a judge.
skills.prompt: Explain the output of the Design Partner Trial Runner harness skill for a hackathon judge. Context: A role-level trial request becomes a report, packet, and access brief without live credentials. Safety: no live integration path; humans review

skills.name: Design Partner Outcome Memo
skills.what_it_proves: A trial request becomes a meeting-ready decision with can-move scope, blocked scope, proof owners, and reviewer routes.
skills.safety_boundary: memo restates blocked claims; never grants access
skills.layman_summary: A trial request becomes a meeting-ready decision with can-move scope, blocked scope, proof owners, and reviewer routes.
skills.example_question: What does Design Partner Outcome Memo show for a hackathon judge?
skills.prompt: Summarize the design-partner outcome memo: what can move, what stays blocked, which proof owners are named, and why no access is granted.

skills.name: Packet Diff Generation
skills.what_it_proves: Scenario packets expose load-bearing differences across risk levels.
skills.safety_boundary: diff is read-only; never mutates packets
skills.layman_summary: Diffs packets across scenarios so drift and risk spread are visible.
skills.example_question: Where do scenarios disagree on production access?
skills.prompt: Explain the output of the Packet Diff Generation harness skill for a hackathon judge. Context: Scenario packets expose load-bearing differences across risk levels. Safety: diff is read-only; never mutates packets

skills.name: Outcome Memo Generation
skills.what_it_proves: The packet becomes a concise human decision surface for what can move and what stays blocked.
skills.safety_boundary: memo restates blocked claims; never grants access
skills.layman_summary: Executive memo after review — still says production is blocked if proof is missing.
skills.example_question: Write a short outcome memo for leadership.
skills.prompt: Explain the output of the Outcome Memo Generation harness skill for a hackathon judge. Context: The packet becomes a concise human decision surface for what can move and what stays blocked. Safety: memo restates blocked claims; never grants access

skills.name: Full judge harness
skills.what_it_proves: One command runs the full public proof path (all scenarios, contract, artifacts).
skills.safety_boundary: read-only; humans approve
skills.layman_summary: Full proof run — all scenarios, gates, and trust receipt in one path.
skills.example_question: Give a one-minute judge tour of the full harness.
skills.prompt: Explain the output of the Full judge harness harness skill for a hackathon judge. Context: One command runs the full public proof path (all scenarios, contract, artifacts). Safety: read-only; humans approve

skills.name: Proof Health / Drift Detection
skills.what_it_proves: Packet assumptions, reviewer gates, and refresh timing are surfaced before access expands.
skills.safety_boundary: health is observational; never auto-refreshes
skills.layman_summary: Scores how stale or incomplete the proof bundle is over time.
skills.example_question: Is proof health drifting for support triage?
skills.prompt: Explain the output of the Proof Health / Drift Detection harness skill for a hackathon judge. Context: Packet assumptions, reviewer gates, and refresh timing are surfaced before access expands. Safety: health is observational; never auto-refreshes

skills.name: Evidence Receipt Ledger
skills.what_it_proves: Tool scope, proof debt, reviewer routes, and cost/procurement controls attach as receipts without weakening the packet decision lock.
skills.safety_boundary: receipts are context only; never weaken locks
skills.layman_summary: Tool scope, proof debt, reviewer routes, and cost/procurement controls attach as receipts without weakening the packet decision lock.
skills.example_question: What does Evidence Receipt Ledger show for a hackathon judge?
skills.prompt: Explain the output of the Evidence Receipt Ledger harness skill for a hackathon judge. Context: Tool scope, proof debt, reviewer routes, and cost/procurement controls attach as receipts without weakening the packet decision lock. Safety: receipts are context only; never weaken locks

skills.name: Artifact Integrity Verification
skills.what_it_proves: Checked-in proof inventory is compared against deterministic generator output.
skills.safety_boundary: regeneration is deterministic; proof bytes locked
skills.layman_summary: Checks public demo artifacts match deterministic harness output.
skills.example_question: Are the generated proof files trustworthy for demo?
skills.prompt: Explain the output of the Artifact Integrity Verification harness skill for a hackathon judge. Context: Checked-in proof inventory is compared against deterministic generator output. Safety: regeneration is deterministic; proof bytes locked

skills.name: AI Spend Review Packet
skills.what_it_proves: A budget-overrun question becomes a Finance and Procurement review packet without approving spend, selecting a provider, or guaranteeing savings.
skills.safety_boundary: spend review packet only; never approves spend
skills.layman_summary: Turns an AI budget-overrun question into Finance and Procurement review artifacts.
skills.example_question: What does IA require before changing AI spend or vendors?
skills.prompt: Summarize the AI Spend Review packet for Finance and Procurement: what evidence is required, what claims stay blocked, and why IA does not approve spend, select a provider, or guarantee savings.

skills.name: Sponsor Proof Readiness
skills.what_it_proves: Sponsor adapters show where live proof can attach while remaining non-executing.
skills.safety_boundary: dry-run by default; no live writes; sponsor cannot grant access
skills.layman_summary: Shows whether Nebius/Tavily/Composio keys are ready for sponsor demos.
skills.example_question: What sponsor integrations are configured vs still needed?
skills.prompt: Explain sponsor proof readiness (Nebius, Tavily, Composio) for this harness: what adds evidence vs what cannot approve access.

skills.layman_summary: Runs policy rules: which scenarios are blocked before any pilot.

help: Type / or + to attach skills as chips, then ask your question — the LLM answers using skill context.

intro.title: What are skills?
intro.blurb: Skills are **ready-made access-review briefs** (not magic buttons). Attach one or more chips, ask a normal question, then **Send**. The AI reads real harness output — who is blocked, what proof is missing — and does **not** grant production access.
steps: Type your question (or use the suggested line under the chips)
Press **Send** — look for “Harness review” in the reply
